plan_steps.action: Scan repository root structure: list all files and directories, read README, package.json (or equivalent manifest), and identify the project type, language, and framework in use.

Inspect source code health: check for linting configs, CI/CD pipeline files, dependency files, open TODOs/FIXMEs, and any obvious code smells or missing best-practice files (e.g. .env.example, CONTRIBUTING.md).
Audit existing test coverage: locate test directories and files, identify the testing framework used, estimate coverage gaps, and flag any untested modules or missing test infrastructure.

Review UI/UX and design assets (if applicable): scan for frontend components, design tokens, style sheets, or asset directories. Identify any inconsistent naming conventions or missing design system elements.
Aggregate all findings from steps 2, 3, and 4 into a structured repository health report. Highlight critical issues, moderate concerns, and positive observations. Produce a prioritized action list.
